questions.question: What units should velocity have?
questions.explanation: Velocity is distance per time = meters per second = m/s

questions.question: If your answer has wrong units, the calculation might still be right.
questions.explanation: If units are wrong, something in your calculation went wrong. Wrong units = wrong answer.

questions.question: You calculate: distance = speed / time. What units do you get?
questions.explanation: m/s divided by s = m/s ÷ s = (m/s) × (1/s) = m/s². Wait, that's acceleration units, not distance! So this formula is WRONG.
